title: BUG: duplicate species eggs (2 T-Rex) — uniqueness race
description: User dug 2 T-Rex eggs. Root cause hypothesis: TryRollUnownedEggSpecies checks OWNED (hatched) dinos only — multiple eggs rolled before the first HATCHES can all pick the same species. Fix: reserve species when the egg DugItemInfo is created (pending set: buried eggs + spilled unhatched eggs), release on hatch/destroy; roll excludes owned+pending. Test: dig two egg batches without hatching -> species differ; also UniqueDinoNoDupes extension.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
FIXED — duplicate-egg-species race (2 T-Rex).

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): TryRollUnownedEggSpecies excluded only OWNED = HATCHED
species (OwnedEggSpecies iterates _dinos, the live DinoControllers). An egg that
was dug but not yet hatched is a live ItemPickup, never in _dinos. FinishDig
resolves the WHOLE batch (GameManager.cs loop calling ResolveDugItem) BEFORE any
pickup spawns/hatches, so two eggs in one batch both saw the same "owned" set and
Random.Range could pick the same unowned species -> 2 T-Rex. Same hole across two
quick digs and for spilled-but-un-hatched eggs.

FIX — pending-species reservation set in GameManager:
- Dictionary<DinoType,int> _reservedEggSpecies (ref-counted, defence in depth).
- TryRollUnownedEggSpecies now excludes OWNED *and* RESERVED species.
- ResolveDugItem RESERVES the species the instant an egg is finalized (after the
  unique re-roll), so a second egg in the same batch / a later dig cannot pick it.
- No-species-remaining fallback: if EggSpeciesAllOwned() (nerf active) -> shard
  (else treasure), else -> FRUIT. Rationale: shards must never drop before every
  egg species is owned, so a batch that merely exhausts the *reserved* pool spills
  fruit rather than leaking an early shard.

RELEASE POINTS:
- HatchEgg: releases the reservation and takes ownership (SpawnDino) in one
  synchronous step — no gap for a duplicate. Direct HatchEgg (no pickup) = no-op.
- ItemPickup.OnDestroy: releases for an egg destroyed WITHOUT hatching (dig
  re-entry, reset); guarded with _hatched flag + !Application.isPlaying so
  play-mode-exit / domain-reload teardown is a safe no-op.
- TestReset: _reservedEggSpecies.Clear().

AUDIT of every egg-DugItemInfo path: FinishDig->ResolveDugItem (reserves) OK;
SpawnRewardPickup now routes egg type through ResolveDugItem (duck reward is only
fruit/treasure today, but the gate is closed); DropTreeFruit makes Fruit only (no
eggs) OK; TestSpawnItem reserves egg species for the pickup lifetime. PEEK during
digging is cosmetic (site-roll tint) and unchanged — two buried eggs at ONE site
now DO get distinct species because FinishDig re-rolls each through the reserving
ResolveDugItem.

TESTS (IntegrationTestCases.Case_UniqueDinoNoDupes extended): resolve a 6-egg
batch with nothing owned -> assert 4 DISTINCT egg species + 2 fruit fallbacks + 0
shard/treasure leak; assert 4 reservations held; reset; assert reservations
cleared; then the original partial/full owned-exclusion assertions. New hooks:
TestResolveDugBatch (mirrors FinishDig, keeps reservations), TestReservedEggSpeciesCount.
TestRollDugItem now releases the egg it samples so the sampling distribution stays
stationary (keeps existing UniqueDinoNoDupes/ShardDropRate/ShardHatchCeremony green).
FeedAndGrow/EggHatch spawn via TestSpawnItem: fruit un-affected; the egg reserves
then releases on hatch.

FILES: Assets/Scripts/Core/GameManager.cs, Assets/Scripts/Overworld/ItemPickup.cs,
Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs. (Not compiled/run here — no Unity
MCP; rerun DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests to verify.)

title: Backhoe pathfinding robustness: any tap = guaranteed route
description: User: 'anywhere player presses, pathfinding should bring them there around rocks/streams/etc — currently takes too much manual clicking.' Likely: stall watchdog honk-gives-up at narrow 1-cell bridges/concave banks; LOS corner-cutting clips water corners; NearestWalkable radius too small for far-water taps. Fix: replan (re-run BFS) on stall instead of giving up (honk only when BFS itself fails); corridor-width LOS (parallel offset samples) so corner cuts never clip obstacles; hug cell centers through 1-cell passages; extend unwalkable-tap clamping. Test: PathfindingAnywhere — 20 random walkable targets across the island incl. across streams/pond, assert arrival within generous timeout, zero give-ups.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Fixed. Root causes: (1) stall watchdog called GiveUp->honk after 1.2s of no progress with no attempt to re-route; (2) LOS corner-cutting (SmoothWaypoints + AdvanceByLineOfSight) sampled only the centerline at 0.25 steps, so a straight shortcut could graze a water/obstacle corner between two walkable cells and wedge the follower; (3) waypoint-arrival threshold was a fixed ~2x arrive distance, so the backhoe cut corners early and clipped the far bank of 1-cell bridges; (4) NearestWalkable ring (r<=8) returned found=false for taps deep in water, making FindPath honk instead of driving to the near shore.

Design:
- REPLAN, never quit: stall watchdog and fully-blocked frame now call TryReplan() (re-run FindPath from the current position toward the same goal). Only honk when FindPath itself returns false, or after MaxReplans=5 replans per journey. _replans resets per SetDestination.
- Corridor-width LOS: new OverworldMap.HasCorridorLineOfSight samples 3 parallel lines (center +/- 0.3 world units perpendicular) and requires all walkable. Used by BOTH SmoothWaypoints and AdvanceByLineOfSight. Through a 1-cell bridge the offset samples land in the flanking water so it fails on purpose -> follower falls back to raw cell-center waypoints (hugs the bridge center).
- Narrow-passage threading: OverworldMap.IsCorridorCell detects a cell pinched by unwalkable cells on both sides of one axis; BackhoeController tightens the waypoint-advance threshold from ~2x to ~1x arrive distance when the current/next waypoint is a corridor.
- Far-water clamp: new NearestWalkable(world, towardFallback, out found) overload; if the ring fails it marches from the tap toward the mover and returns the first walkable cell (the near shore). FindPath uses it, passing fromWorld. A deep pond tap now drives to shore instead of no-op.
- Facing smoother untouched.

Test hook: BackhoeController.TestGiveUpCount (internal) counts honk-give-ups (initial FindPath fail + GiveUp).

Test: new Case_PathfindingAnywhere (Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs, registered after RoamTapToMove, 120s). Seeds System.Random(0xD1D0), Fisher-Yates over all walkable cells, greedily picks 18 targets >=6 cells apart and >=6 from start (asserts span >=24x24). MoveTo each; asserts arrival on the exact target cell, walkable, and TestGiveUpCount stays at baseline (zero give-ups). Then parks far from the pond and routes a move AT a deep interior pond cell, asserting near-shore clamp (walkable, != water cell, moved >0.25 closer, no give-up).

Files changed: Assets/Scripts/Overworld/BackhoeController.cs, Assets/Scripts/Overworld/OverworldMap.cs, Assets/Scripts/Testing/IntegrationTestCases.cs. Did not touch SceneBuilder/StreamNetwork/DuckController or any other locked lane.

Not run here (no Unity MCP in this session): please run DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests and confirm PathfindingAnywhere passes with RoamTapToMove/MoundToDig/FacingCorrectness/FacingStability green, then drive around live tapping across the streams/pond to confirm one tap = one arrival.

title: BUG: streams fragment into short disconnected segments
description: User report: 'random little short blue lines that dont connect' — streams must read as continuous ribbons from one map edge/feature to another (coast->pond, coast->coast). Likely causes: meander walk terminating early on collisions, connectivity healing converting segments to bridges too aggressively, or courses overwriting each other. Rewrite carving to guarantee continuous courses; verify visually + StreamsConnectivity extension asserting course continuity (adjacent cells) and endpoint validity.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. Reworked stream carving in SceneBuilder from a biased random walk (which dead-ended and fragmented) to A* (AStarStream): each of 3 ribbons is laid as ONE unbroken 4-connected route from a coastal grass source to the pond. A* routes AROUND trees/rocks/meadow/start-clearing (RoutableForStream excludes them) and NEVER erases them; a path band it must cross is stamped 'B' (bridge) at a stiff cost so crossings stay rare and perpendicular. Courses are ordered coast->pond and store ALL cells (incl. bridged ones); walkability comes from the tilemap, so the mandatory EnsureStreamConnectivity healer still bridges ('S'->'B') any stream-cut region without breaking course continuity (the bridged cell was already in its course). StreamsConnectivity test extended: asserts per-course 4-adjacent continuity, coastal source + pond mouth endpoints, and full island walkable connectivity.

title: Unique hatch + egg nerf + shard drop
description: No duplicate species ever hatch: dig egg rolls exclude owned species; once ALL rollable species owned, egg weight drops to 20% of current and the freed 80% becomes EGG SHARD items (new diggable, sparkly shell piece art, flies to nest like treasure does to counter). Owned-species egg that would roll -> shard instead. ShardDropRate + UniqueDinoNoDupes tests.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Implemented. (a) Egg rolls exclude owned entirely: GameManager.ResolveDugItem reassigns each dug egg a uniformly-random UNOWNED egg species (original 4 only); TryRollUnownedEggSpecies. (b) When all 4 egg species owned, DigModeController.RollItem cuts egg weight to 20% and routes the freed 80% to new ItemType.Shard; any residual owned-species egg also converts to a shard in ResolveDugItem (so 0 dupe eggs ever). (c) Shards dig+fly like treasure via ItemPickup.Shard -> GameManager.CollectShard, which routes to GameEvents.NestTargetProvider (falls back to meadow center, then treasure counter, then up), increments SaveData.ShardCount, and raises GameEvents.ShardCollected. Tests: UniqueDinoNoDupes, ShardDropRate (direct roll hooks TestRollDugItem/TestEggSpeciesAllOwned).

title: Design 5 shard-exclusive dino species
description: Pick 5 kid-favorite species distinct in silhouette+color from the 4 owned (candidates: Pteranodon/teal, Ankylosaurus/red, Spinosaurus/yellow-green, Parasaurolophus/pink, Mosasaurus/navy... final call with art tests). Deliverable: names, colors, one-line dance + description each, DinoType enum extension plan.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Roster designed + implemented. docs/dino-roster.md lists all 9 (name/hex/egg pattern/dance/one-liner). Egg-hatchable: TRex green, Triceratops orange, Brachiosaurus blue, Stegosaurus purple. Shard-exclusive (new): Pteranodon teal (WingFlap), Ankylosaurus red (TailClub), Spinosaurus yellow-green (SailWiggle), Parasaurolophus pink (CrestToot), Velociraptor sky-grey (SpinHop). DinoType extended to 4-8 (index<4 = egg-hatchable, load-bearing order); DanceType +5; added DinoSpecies helper (EggHatchableCount/IsEggHatchable). PlaceholderArtGenerator now emits all 9 blobs+eggs and the shard sprite, and builds 9 DinoDefinition assets.

title: Stage-transform art pipeline (adult->kid->baby via img2img chain)
description: Extend Tools/generate_sprites.py: per dino, img2img the ADULT front view to 'same character, younger kid version, slightly bigger head, rounder body' and again to 'baby: big head, stubby limbs, huge eyes'; then rotate each stage front via the proven img2img rotation chain (5 gens + 3 mirrors). Keeps identity consistent because every image derives from the same source. Acceptance: baby/kid/adult fronts of one pilot dino (trex) visually same character.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Extended generate_sprites.py + slice_sprites.py with a 'stages' category. Per dino, img2img de-ages the existing adult raw S view (raw/<base>_S.png) -> kid_S -> baby_S (STAGE_TRANSFORM prompts), then rotates each stage's S via the existing turnaround_rotate_prompt chain (SE/E/NE/N gens + SW/W/NW slicer mirrors). Idempotent (per-file skip), resumable under --only (stages exempt from --only force so a re-run resumes from the failed image), retries via _attempt. Slicer writes Generated/<outdir>/<stage>_<DIR>.png; adult keeps <base>_<DIR>.png. ~10 gens/dino. Pilot trex verified: baby/kid/adult are the same character; strengthened the baby prompt (huge chibi head, tiny body) after first pass read too subtle.

title: EPIC: Unique dinos + egg shard nest progression (9 species, no duplicates)
description: One dino per species ever. After a species is hatched, its egg drop weight drops to 20%; the freed weight becomes EGG SHARDS (new diggable). Shards accumulate visually: a nest in the meadow pen pieces an egg together (shard-by-shard growth). At 20 shards, a NEW species hatches from the nest: camera zooms, celebration, tap to join team (becomes buddy). 5 new shard-only species. Save v3.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: All children closed except walk-anim art usage; epic core complete: uniqueness, shards, nest, ceremony, 9-species art all landed. 35/35 suite green.

title: EPIC: Walk animation (2 walk frames + 1 idle, 8 directions)
description: Per dino per stage per direction: idle frame (exists = current directional sprite) + 2 stride frames (left-leg / right-leg forward). Consistency strategy: img2img pose-edit each EXISTING directional sprite ('same character mid-stride, left leg forward') so identity/angle never drift; mirror E/NE/SE sets to W/NW/SW like the base sprites. Runtime: simple 3-frame cycler in DinoController/BackhoeController-style, no Animator asset needed.

title: EPIC: Distinct growth-stage art (baby/kid/adult per dino)
description: Replace uniform scaling with real per-stage sprites: baby (big head, stubby), kid, adult — same cute toy-box style. Applies to all 9 dino species (4 current + 5 shard-exclusive). Art via img2img stage-transform chain from each dino's adult front view to keep character identity consistent.

title: Backhoe movement animation (wheel-roll frames, 8 dirs)
description: 2 roll frames per direction via img2img (wheels rotated to a different spoke angle + slight suspension bob, everything else frozen — vehicle variant of the stride prompt). 5 canonical dirs x 2 = 10 gens. BackhoeController gets the same idle/A/idle/B cycler while driving; test BackhoeRollCycles.

title: Bridge tiles: stone-grey visual where paths cross streams
description: Only paths may cross streams, and the crossing must read as a BRIDGE: new stone-grey bridge tile (PlaceholderArtGenerator + PlaceholderLibrary.BridgeTile), painted at 'B' cells instead of plain path tile. Walkable as before.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. Added PlaceholderLibrary.BridgeTile + PlaceholderArtGenerator.WriteBridge: a stone-grey cobble deck over a blue water diamond (grey 150/150/162 cobbles with light/dark specks, masked to the iso diamond) — distinct from tan path and blue water. Generator now creates the 'Bridge' tile and wires lib.BridgeTile. PaintMap paints 'B' cells with BridgeTile on the GROUND layer (no water tile) so they stay walkable like a path and read as a bridge over the channel.

title: BUG: ducks not swimming ON the stream water
description: Ducks should visibly float down the stream channel. Verify course->world alignment (cell centers), sorting above water tile, bob baseline, and spawn only on actual continuous courses (depends on stream fix).

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Fixed. With continuous coast->pond courses, ducks now spawn at the COAST end (route[0]) and swim the full course. World positions are course cell centers (StreamNetwork.CellCenter/grid.GetCellCenterWorld); sorting stays order 10 (above water tile 1, below backhoe 12/dinos 15); bob amplitude unchanged at 0.05 to keep them on the channel. Passing UNDER bridges: DuckController now takes the OverworldMap and flags each course cell that is walkable (a 'B' bridge deck); the duck fades its sprite alpha to 0 while crossing a bridged cell and back to 1 on the far side (deck is on the ground layer below all sprites, so a fade is the simplest correct read). Catch restores full alpha. DuckCatch test unaffected.

title: Save v3 + migration (shards, owned-species, nest state)
description: SaveData v3: ShardCount, no-dup ownership derivable from Dinos list, nest state. v2 saves migrate cleanly. SaveRoundtrip extended.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: SaveData -> v3: added ShardCount + NestSpeciesQueue (List<DinoType>); ParadeDone retained. CurrentVersion=3. Migration is default-based (JsonUtility leaves absent fields at defaults). Fixed a latent bug: the buddy-fallback legacy check used <CurrentVersion, which would have mis-migrated v2 saves after the v3 bump; now keyed off new SaveData.BuddyFieldVersion=2. SaveRoundtrip extended to roundtrip ShardCount + version and to assert a hand-written v2 JSON migrates with ShardCount defaulting to 0 and NestSpeciesQueue non-null.

title: Shard hatch ceremony: zoom + celebrate + tap to join
description: At 20 shards: camera eases to the nest, egg wobble+crack, NEW species (random unowned from the 5 shard-only) hatches with confetti+sting; it waits by the nest; tapping it makes it a buddy (joins team). Shards reset, next egg begins if unowned species remain. ShardHatchCeremony test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Starting with placeholder species art (9 blob definitions exist); real art from bl6.2 swaps in via importer with no code change.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Shard-hatch ceremony landed.

- GameState.Ceremony added: blocks dig entry + backhoe move during the zoom; taps on the new dino still route (OnTap does collider-tap first, state-gates only backhoe/tree). CameraFollow gained EnterFocus/ExitFocus (reuses the EaseInOutCubic dig-transition ease; parks on the nest, GameConfig.CeremonyOrthoSize).
- GameManager: CollectShard -> TryBeginCeremony when ShardCount >= ShardsPerHatch AND a shard species is unowned. Sequence: state Ceremony -> camera eases to nest -> NestController.PlayHatch (egg shows whole, ShakeRotation wobble+crack, sparkle) + confetti + Hatch/Roar sting -> spawn the random UNOWNED shard species (DinoType 4-8) at the nest, Baby, forced RESIDENT (waits in the meadow). ShardCount -= ShardsPerHatch (remainder kept), save written. Camera eases back after ~3s OR on tap. First tap promotes the baby to buddy via the existing NotifyDinoTapped flow and ends the ceremony early (ceremony state does not eat the tap).
- Added GameManager.AnyShardSpeciesUnowned; ResolveDugItem now gates shards on it: once all 5 shard species are owned, shards (and would-be eggs) downgrade to treasure -> 'shards stop dropping'. Existing Unique/ShardDropRate tests unaffected (they own only egg species).
- TestReset clears ceremony state (via TestForceRoam).
- Test: ShardHatchCeremony (set ShardCount=19, collect 1 -> Ceremony fires, new unowned shard species at nest as resident, tap -> buddy + state Roam; run 2 with all 9 owned -> no ceremony, 0 shard rolls).

title: Nest in meadow + shard egg assembly visual
description: Nest prop in the meadow pen; as shards accumulate (save v3 ShardCount), the egg in the nest visibly assembles: 5 build states (0/5/10/15/20 shards) via generated egg-fragment art or masked reveal. NestAssembly test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Nest + shard egg-assembly visual landed.

- PlaceholderLibrary: added NestSprite (twig-ring bowl) + EggAssemblySprites[5]; PlaceholderArtGenerator draws nest_base + egg_assembly_0..4 (cracked fragments piecing into a whole cream egg at 0/25/50/75/100%) and wires them in.
- MeadowArea.NestWorld exposes the NE interior-corner cell (unique, stable spot).
- SceneBuilder.CreateMeadow now builds the Nest prop (base + egg + sparkle) at that cell and wires GameManager._nest.
- New NestController.cs: registers GameEvents.NestTargetProvider (shards fly to the egg), listens to ShardCollected, advances the egg-assembly sprite by ShardCount thresholds (per/(states-1) step of GameConfig.ShardsPerHatch=20), punch-scale + sparkle on each arrival. Persists nothing (reads ShardCount from GameManager). Real art can replace the PlaceholderLibrary fields with no code change.
- Test: NestAssembly (collect shards via TestSpawnItem/CollectShard -> assembly index steps 0/1/2/3 at thresholds, egg sprite changes).

title: Art for 5 new species (turnarounds + eggs)
description: Existing pipeline: front + img2img rotations + egg per species (~7 gens each, ~35 total). Then stage + walk art via the other epics' pipelines.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Scope extended to include baby/kid stage art for the 5 new species (stage pipeline from 00x.1 now available) — ~85 generations total.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
Art set for the 5 shard-exclusive species complete + wired.

GENERATION (Tools/generate_sprites.py, google/gemini-2.5-flash-image via OpenRouter):
Per species = 1 front(S) + 4 img2img rotations + 1 egg + 10 stage images (kid/baby S de-age chain + 8 rotations) = 16. Gated each species (front+egg+baby via Read) before rotations.
- Pteranodon (teal): grounded on 2 legs, folded wings, crest. Passed first try.
- Ankylosaurus (red): armored body + tail club. Passed first try.
- Spinosaurus (yellow-green): first front had a weak/tiny sail (failed silhouette-distinct gate) -> strengthened subject prompt to a HUGE full-back sail, regenerated adult front + derived kid/baby S. Second attempt: unmistakable giant sail. (only notable retry)
- Parasaurolophus (pink): prominent tube crest + duck bill. Passed first try.
- Velociraptor (grey): bipedal raptor + tail. Passed first try.
All 5 eggs passed first try (teal wing-streaks / red armored-pebble / olive sail-stripe / pink curved crest / grey zig-zag flecks).

Total API calls: 83 (20 gate + 3 spinosaurus regen + 60 rotation/stage batch). 0 failures. Batch is idempotent/resumable.

FILES: raw at Tools/raw/{species}_*.png + egg_{teal,red,olive,pink,grey}.png. Sliced to Assets/Art/Generated/{species}/ (24 PNGs each: 8 adult {species}_DIR + 8 baby_DIR + 8 kid_DIR) and Assets/Art/Generated/eggs/egg_{color}.png. (no .meta written; Unity generates them)

WIRING (Assets/Scripts/Editor/GeneratedArtImporter.cs):
- CharacterFolders += pteranodon, ankylosaurus, spinosaurus, parasaurolophus, velociraptor (8-dir + baby/kid stage sets, shared per-actor PPU).
- Dinos[] += 5 DinoWire entries: EggFile mapping Pteranodon->egg_teal, Ankylosaurus->egg_red, Spinosaurus->egg_olive, Parasaurolophus->egg_pink, Velociraptor->egg_grey.
- eggs[] config array extended so the 5 new eggs get the same ItemTargetH PPU as the originals.
- Stage arrays identical to the 4 originals; importer stays tolerant of missing art/defs. DinoDefinition.cs untouched. GameManager/SceneBuilder/PlaceholderArtGenerator/Testing untouched.

All 9 Dino_*.asset present; on next 'DinoDigger/Import Generated Art' run all 9 species wire fully (adult+baby+kid+egg).

MANUAL REGEN NEEDED: none.

title: Catch-a-duck reward
description: Duck has ITappable collider (existing tap router picks it up, no GameManager changes): tap -> quack + flap-away arc (flying frame, fast climb, despawn) leaving a fruit or treasure pickup (50/50) where it was. DuckCatch test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Catch-a-duck: Duck has CircleCollider2D + ITappable; OnTapped -> quack (reuses the wired Honk clip; a new AudioConfig Quack slot would be silent since the art/audio importer is out-of-lane), flap-away arc (swaps to duck_fly, rises+accelerates offscreen via reverse gravity, wing-wobble, despawns), and drops a 50/50 fruit-or-treasure pickup at the catch spot through new GameManager.SpawnRewardPickup() (public hook wrapping CreatePickup; clamps landing to nearest walkable). Test Case_DuckCatch: force-spawn via DuckController.TestForceSpawnDuck, route a tap, assert caught + reward appears + duck despawns. Needs scene rebuild + Unity test rerun.

title: Duck actor: drift, art, spawn cycle
description: Duck art via pipeline (small turnaround: E/W enough for stream drift + a flying frame). DuckController: spawns at a stream head every 20-40s, drifts along the stream course slowly, despawns at the end. Max 2 concurrent ducks.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Duck art via NEW standalone Tools/generate_ducks.py (reuses the OpenRouter Gemini API pattern + slice_sprites chroma_key/trim; never edits generate_sprites.py). Generated duck_E (side, faces E; runtime flipX for W) + duck_fly, magenta bg, chunky white toy duck -> Assets/Art/Generated/duck/{duck_E,duck_fly}.png (confirmed transparent). DuckController.cs: spawner (every 20-40s, max 2 alive, Roam-only) spawns a Duck at a random stream head; Duck drifts cell-to-cell along the course at 0.5 u/s with a gentle bob, faces travel dir, despawns at the mouth. Sorting 10 (above water 1, below backhoe 12/dinos 15). SceneBuilder creates+wires DuckSpawner and configures the duck sprite importers (~0.5 world units). Needs scene rebuild to appear.

title: Streams on the map (unwalkable, bridged at paths)
description: SceneBuilder: 2-3 one-tile streams meandering from coast to pond/coast; water tiles (unwalkable); auto-bridge where they cross path bands (bridge tile stays walkable). MUST keep the island fully connected — add a post-gen BFS connectivity check that re-carves a bridge if any walkable region is orphaned. StreamsConnectivity test.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Streams + connectivity implemented in SceneBuilder (CarveStreams/PaintMap 'S'->water,'B'->bridge/EnsureStreamConnectivity BFS+bridge-or-dissolve), new StreamNetwork.cs stores ordered courses on the Grid. Test Case_StreamsConnectivity added. Needs scene rebuild + Unity test rerun to verify.

title: Runtime walk-cycle player (idle/stride-A/stride-B)
description: Frame cycler in DinoController (and shared util): while moving alternate strideA/strideB ~6fps, idle frame when still; per-direction frame sets from DinoDefinition. Test WalkAnimCycles: sprite alternates while moving, holds idle when settled (extends DinoIdleStable).

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
DONE. DinoDefinition: per-stage stride arrays (WalkA/WalkB + Baby/Kid variants, 8 each) + StrideSprites(stage, phase) which returns null when no matching art exists AND enforces set-consistency: strides always come from the same art set the idle frame comes from (a stage that falls back to adult idles uses adult strides; a stage with its own idles never mixes in adult strides). GeneratedArtImporter: imports walk frames at the actor's shared PPU and fills the arrays (null when files absent; summary line now reports n/48 strides per dino).

DinoController: TickWalkAnim advances a 4-beat cycle idle->A->idle->B at 6 fps scaled by actual move speed (clamped 0.5-2x of DinoFollowSpeed), driven only from MoveTowards so every mover (follow/stroll/travel/eat/parade) animates; ApplySprite overlays the stride frame for beats 1/3 using the CURRENT facing so direction changes stay in phase; EnterIdle resets to the idle frame. Zero behavior change when stride arrays are null (cycler early-outs; every other dino + trex baby/kid stay static).

Test WalkAnimCycles (pinned to trex ADULT on purpose — hard-fails if the import regresses rather than skip-passing): spawns a Big trex buddy, drives the backhoe ~3s, asserts >=2 distinct stride sprites rendered while moving + idle beats between them, then asserts the settled dino is back on the plain idle facing frame.

Compile-verified (runtime + editor assemblies build clean against Unity 6000.0.61 references via csc). NOT yet run in-editor: Unity MCP was out of scope for this task — next Unity session should run DinoDigger/Import Generated Art (wires the new sprites + regenerates .meta for the walk PNGs), then DinoDigger/Run Integration Tests. Note: I also fixed a variable-name collision (batch) another agent's TestResolveDugBatch edit introduced at IntegrationTestCases.cs:739 — rename only, no logic change.

title: Generate walk frames for all dinos/stages
description: 4 current dinos x 3 stages x 5 canonical dirs x 2 strides = 120 gens (+5 new species later ~150 more). Batch with resume support; budget checkpoint with Greg before firing the full batch.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Pilot approved: trex adult only (10 generations). Full ~270-gen batch awaits Greg's sign-off after reviewing the pilot in motion.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Greg approved the full batch. Applying the pilot's camera-freeze prompt technique globally + tightened back-detail freeze for N-facing frames; trex N pair re-rolled too.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Rescoped by Greg: trex (all stages) + triceratops (all stages) complete — measured cost $1.37 for 38 images ($0.0361/image). Remaining 7 species (~210 images, ~$7.59 measured-rate) stay gated pending future approval. Suite 37/37 green with walk cycler live.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Greg approved the remaining 7 species (~210 gens, ~$7.59 measured rate) plus backhoe roll animation.

title: Per-stage sprite sets in DinoDefinition + growth swap
description: DinoDefinition: WalkSprites per stage (Baby/Kid/Big arrays); importer wires them; DinoController swaps sprite set on grow (keep scale factors ~1.0/1.15/1.3 much subtler now that art carries the growth). GrowthStageArt test: sprite set changes at each growth.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Wired per-stage sprite sets + growth swap. DinoDefinition: added BabySprites[8]/KidSprites[8] (WalkSprites stays the adult/Big set, name unchanged for back-compat); StageSprites(GrowthStage) returns the matching set and falls back to WalkSprites when a stage array is empty; added GetSprite(dir,stage). GeneratedArtImporter: configures baby_/kid_ textures at the SAME per-actor PPU as the adult set (heights within ~5%, so StageScales carries size, art carries shape) and fills def.BabySprites/KidSprites from <folder>/<stage>_<DIR>.png (null when absent -> adult fallback; missing stage files not flagged as errors). DinoController: caches _activeSprites for the current stage and swaps the whole set when Stage changes (ApplySprite lazily re-swaps; ForceStage + GrowPoof force an immediate swap); facing indexer now draws from _activeSprites. GameConfig StageScales softened 1.0/1.35/1.8 -> 1.0/1.15/1.3 (both GameConfig.cs default AND the serialized GameConfig.asset). Tests: added GrowthStageArt (grows Baby->Kid->Big via ForceStage, asserts rendered sprite == the stage array's sprite for the current facing, and baby-front != adult-front); FeedAndGrow reads scales dynamically from config (auto-adapts to softened values) + now also asserts the baby base scale. New DinoController test hooks: TestSprite, TestActiveSprites, TestStageDirSprite. NOTE: GrowthStageArt's baby!=adult assertion requires 'DinoDigger/Import Generated Art' to have run so the stage sets are wired.

title: Stride-pose art pipeline (2 walk frames per direction via img2img pose edit)
description: img2img each existing directional sprite: 'exact same character and camera angle, mid-stride, left leg forward' and 'right leg forward'. Mirror E/NE/SE to W/NW/SW. Pilot one dino/stage, verify no identity drift, then batch.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text:
PILOT DONE (trex adult). Pipeline: new 'strides' category in Tools/generate_sprites.py (STRIDES=walkA/walkB, stride_prompt) + slice_strides in Tools/slice_sprites.py; documented in Tools/README.md.

PROMPT TECHNIQUE THAT WORKED: img2img the existing facing raw with 'EXACT SAME character ... captured MID-STRIDE, {left|right} leg striding FORWARD, other leg pushing BACK, knees bent' + a hard block: 'change ONLY the legs and feet; head/body/arms/tail stay EXACTLY as in the reference, SAME position on canvas, SAME scale' + (critical, added after N failed twice) an explicit CAMERA ANGLE clause: 'if the reference shows the character from BEHIND, the new image must ALSO show it from behind with NO face visible' — without it the model spun the N back view around to a front view.

GENERATION LOG (10 frames, 16 calls total): S/SE/E/NE first try except: walkB_S (1 retry: head tilt drift), walkB_SE (1 retry: lost the tail), walkA_N+walkB_N (2 retries each; final frames accepted). CAVEAT for review: the N (back-view) strides redraw the back detail (center spine bumps, tail swept right) vs the idle N (left ridge, tail left). A and B agree with each other so the walk itself is smooth; a small pop shows on N idle<->walk transitions only. Max-retry budget reached; flag if it bothers you in motion and we can re-roll N with a tighter seed before the y85.2 batch.

TRIM ALIGNMENT: slicer chroma-keys idle+walkA+walkB together per facing and crops all three to the UNION of their alpha bboxes in raw-pixel space (idle PNG re-written onto the shared canvas). Identical canvas per facing + fixed feet baseline (verified bottoms within ~14px on ~720px sprites) = no hop on frame swap. Naming: adult walkA_<DIR>.png / walkB_<DIR>.png (unprefixed like adult idles); stages <stage>_walkA_<DIR>.png. Importer imports strides at the actor's shared PPU.

SAFETY: un-piloted dinos' stride entries are gated with stride_stages=[] so a full generate run cannot fire the ~260-gen batch before your sign-off (y85.2 flips them to [None,'kid','baby']).

title: Generate stage art: 4 current dinos
description: Run the stage pipeline for trex/triceratops/brachiosaurus/stegosaurus: 2 stage transforms + 2x4 img2img rotations each (~12 gens/dino, ~48 total). Slice to Assets/Art/Generated/<dino>/<stage>_<DIR>.png.

comments.author: Greg Back
comments.text: Generated + sliced stage art for all 4 dinos (trex, triceratops, brachiosaurus, stegosaurus). 40 raw gens total, ALL succeeded on attempt 1, zero retries. Trex piloted + eyeballed first: identity rock-solid across baby/kid/adult; baby prompt strengthened once for a clearer big-head/tiny-body read (regen resumed cleanly, kept the good kid). Eyeballed baby-vs-adult S for all 4: same character, clear growth arc (strong for trex/trike/brachio; subtler for stego whose adult art is already very round, but head-ratio shifts + scale multiplier carry it). Sliced to 16 sprites/dino (kid_+baby_ x 8 dirs) under Assets/Art/Generated/<dino>/.

title: EPIC: Streams and ducks
description: A few 1-tile-wide streams crossing the island (unwalkable water, bridged where paths cross). Ducks drift along stream paths slowly, despawn at the end. Tap (catch) a duck -> it flaps away with a quack and leaves a fruit or treasure where it was.
